note_data.title: Genetic technology
keyPoints: All living organisms are made of cells that use ATP from respiration for energy-requiring processes
Eukaryotic cells contain a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles, while prokaryotic cells do not contain membrane-bound organelles
Plant cells contain unique organelles: chloroplasts (site of photosynthesis), vacuoles (provide support through turgidity), cell walls (made of cellulose microfibrils), and plasmodesmata
Animal cells may contain cilia (hair-like structures in 9+2 formation for movement) and microvilli (finger-like projections that increase surface area)
Prokaryotic cells are unicellular, 1-5μm in diameter, have circular DNA, peptidoglycan cell walls, and 70S ribosomes
Eukaryotic cells have linear DNA, larger 80S ribosomes, and various membrane-bound organelles including nucleus, mitochondria, and Golgi apparatus
Viruses are non-living structures consisting of nucleic acid (DNA or RNA) enclosed in a protein capsid, sometimes with a phospholipid envelope

definitions.definition: A more detailed structure of cells that can be obtained by using a microscope
Cells that contain a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles
A protective protein coat that encloses nucleic acid in viruses
Unicellular organisms typically 1-5μm in diameter that do not contain membrane-bound organelles

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give a sequence of steps or detailed account of the process
Give a plausible biological explanation using your knowledge

modelAnswer.question: Describe the process of using PCR to amplify a specific gene. [6]
modelAnswer.answer: DNA sample is heated to 95°C to denature the double-stranded DNA and separate the strands. Temperature is lowered to 55°C to allow primers to anneal to complementary sequences flanking the target gene. Temperature is raised to 72°C for DNA polymerase to extend the primers and synthesize new DNA strands. This cycle is repeated 25-30 times to exponentially amplify the target sequence. Each cycle doubles the number of target DNA molecules. Millions of copies of the specific gene are produced.
markBreakdown: Mark 3: Extension at 72°C by DNA polymerase

commonMistakes: Confusing the ribosome sizes - prokaryotes have 70S ribosomes while eukaryotes have 80S ribosomes in cytoplasm
Forgetting that both mitochondria and chloroplasts contain small circular DNA and 70S ribosomes
Mixing up cell wall compositions - prokaryotes have peptidoglycan, plants have cellulose, fungi have chitin
examinerTips: Remember that centrioles are involved in cell division and are absent from most plant cells
Know that plasmodesmata allow communication between adjoining plant cells by passing through cell walls
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Gel electrophoresis setup showing wells at negative electrode, DNA fragments separated by size with smaller fragments near positive electrode, and bands visible under UV light
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to describe the process of genetic engineering in bacteria and explain advantages of insulin production using this method
N/D 2022 P1 Q12 (1 mark) — multiple choice question on identifying the correct sequence of steps in genetic engineering
M/J 2022 P2 Q5(c) (4 marks) — students had to explain ethical concerns surrounding genetic modification of crops
N/D 2023 P2 Q3(b) (5 marks) — describe how PCR is used to amplify DNA and state two applications of this technique
M/J 2021 P2 Q6 (8 marks) — comprehensive question covering gene therapy, its process, and potential risks and benefits

workedExample.question: Describe the process of genetic engineering to produce human insulin in bacteria. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Identify what genetic engineering involves - transferring genes between organisms
Step 2: Describe isolation - human insulin gene is cut from chromosome using restriction enzymes
Step 3: Explain vector preparation - plasmid DNA cut with same restriction enzyme to create compatible sticky ends
Step 4: Describe gene insertion - insulin gene inserted into plasmid using ligase enzyme
Step 5: Explain transformation - recombinant plasmid inserted into bacterial cell
Step 6: Describe production - bacteria reproduce and express insulin gene to produce human insulin
workedExample.answer: The human insulin gene is isolated from a chromosome using restriction enzymes that cut at specific sequences. A bacterial plasmid is cut using the same restriction enzyme, creating complementary sticky ends. The insulin gene is inserted into the plasmid using ligase enzyme to form recombinant DNA. This recombinant plasmid is introduced into bacterial cells through transformation. The bacteria are cultured and reproduce rapidly, expressing the insulin gene to produce human insulin that can be harvested and purified for medical use.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark each for: gene isolation using restriction enzymes; plasmid preparation; gene insertion using ligase; transformation into bacteria; bacterial reproduction/culture; insulin production/expression

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused genetic engineering with selective breeding' — always specify that genetic engineering involves transferring genes between different species, not just selecting traits within a species

Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently omitted the role of enzymes in genetic engineering' — always mention restriction enzymes for cutting DNA and ligase for joining DNA when describing the process

Examiner Report 2023: 'Candidates often provided vague ethical arguments without specific examples' — when discussing ethics, give concrete examples like antibiotic resistance genes in GM crops or religious objections to human gene therapy
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw the genetic engineering process showing: 1) Human cell with chromosome containing target gene, 2) Gene being cut out by restriction enzymes (shown as scissors), 3) Bacterial plasmid being cut at the same restriction site, 4) Gene insertion into plasmid forming recombinant DNA (sticky ends clearly labeled), 5) Transformed bacterial cell containing recombinant plasmid, 6) Bacterial reproduction producing multiple copies. Include labels for restriction enzymes, ligase, sticky ends, plasmid, and recombinant DNA.
note_data.quickRecap: All living organisms are made of cells that use ATP for energy. Eukaryotic cells have a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles, while prokaryotic cells lack these features and are much smaller. Plant and animal cells have specific organelles suited to their functions, and viruses are non-living structures with nucleic acid in protein coats.
